blocks.body: Wish / If only help you talk about regret, a desired change, or irritation about a present, past, or future-related situation.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"Wish and if only are close in meaning: they show that we want reality to be different. Often this means regret, dissatisfaction, or a desire for change.","rules":[{"label":"PRESENT REGRET","color":"emerald","text":"After wish, we often use the past simple for an unreal present situation.","example":"I wish I knew the answer."},{"label":"PAST REGRET","color":"blue","text":"For regret about the past, we use the past perfect.","example":"If only I had listened to you."},{"label":"DESIRED CHANGE","color":"amber","text":"Wish + would often expresses irritation or a wish for someone or something to change.","example":"I wish the neighbours would be quieter."}]}

{"title":"1. Main patterns","intro":"Three patterns are especially common:","items":[{"label":"Present","title":"wish + past simple","subtitle":"I wish I had more time."},{"label":"Past","title":"wish + past perfect","subtitle":"I wish I had studied harder."},{"label":"Change in behaviour","title":"wish + would","subtitle":"I wish he would answer my messages."},{"label":"If only","title":"same grammar, stronger feeling","subtitle":"If only we lived closer."}]}

{"title":"2. Main uses","sections":[{"label":"Present regret","color":"emerald","description":"We say that the present situation is not as we want it to be.","examples":[{"en":"I wish I were taller.","ua":"I wish I were taller."},{"en":"If only we had more space in this flat.","ua":"If only we had more space in this flat."}]},{"label":"Past regret","color":"blue","description":"We regret something we did or did not do in the past.","examples":[{"en":"I wish I had called her earlier.","ua":"I wish I had called her earlier."},{"en":"If only they had checked the tickets first.","ua":"If only they had checked the tickets first."}]},{"label":"Irritation or desired change","color":"amber","description":"Wish + would often sounds like a complaint or a wish for a change in behaviour or events.","examples":[{"en":"I wish it would stop raining.","ua":"I wish it would stop raining."},{"en":"I wish my phone would work properly.","ua":"I wish my phone would work properly."}],"note":"Do not use this form for a stable fact about yourself such as I wish I would be taller."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using the present tense after wish for present regret.","wrong":"I wish I know the answer.","right":"I wish I knew the answer.","hint":"For unreal present meaning after wish, we usually use the past simple."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Mixing up past regret and present regret.","wrong":"I wish I knew the answer yesterday.","right":"I wish I had known the answer yesterday.","hint":"For the past, you need the past perfect."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using would for a fixed personal fact.","wrong":"I wish I would be taller.","right":"I wish I were taller.","hint":"Wish + would is better for behaviour or events that can change."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Wish + past simple = regret about the present.","Wish + past perfect = regret about the past.","Wish + would = a wish for change, often with irritation.","If only uses the same grammar but often sounds more emotional."]}
